chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C. P. No. D-168 of 2023 Date Order with signature of Judge FRESH CASE. 1. For orders on Misc. No.612/2023. 2. For orders on Misc. No.613/2023. 3. For hearing of main case. 11.01.2023. Petitioner Mst. Samira Mahamadi is present in person. ------ YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, J. - The Petitioner has invoked the jurisdiction of this Court under Article 199 of the Constitution, seeking that action be initiated against the Respondents, being the Inspector General of Police, Sindh, the Administrator of the Pakistan Defence Officers Housing Authority, Karachi, and the Chief Executive Officer of the Cantonment Board, Clifton, under the Sindh Sound System (Regulation) Act, 2015 for their alleged inaction towards securing the fundamental rights of the Petitioner by failing to implement that statute. The Petitioner, who is present in person, contends that the aforesaid statute had been violated by private persons who reside within the vicinity of the Petitioner’s residence situated at DHA Phase-I, Karachi and that despite the transgression being brought to the notice of the Respondents, no action was taken. Having examined the Petition, it is apparent that the pleadings are bereft of any particular of the alleged violation and the only discernible indication thereof arises from copies of certain letters said to have been addressed by the Petitioner to the Respondents on 31.12.2022 and thereafter, which reflect that the grievance is in relation to a private party at a private home said to have been organized within the vicinity of the Petitioner’s residence on the occasion of New Year’s Eve, with the Petitioner seeking that action be taken under the statute on that single score.

The Petitioner, who is present in person, contends that the aforesaid statute had been violated by private persons who reside within the vicinity of the Petitioner’s residence situated at DHA Phase-I, Karachi and that despite the transgression being brought to the notice of the Respondents, no action was taken. Having examined the Petition, it is apparent that the pleadings are bereft of any particular of the alleged violation and the only discernible indication thereof arises from copies of certain letters said to have been addressed by the Petitioner to the Respondents on 31.12.2022 and thereafter, which reflect that the grievance is in relation to a private party at a private home said to have been organized within the vicinity of the Petitioner’s residence on the occasion of New Year’s Eve, with the Petitioner seeking that action be taken under the statute on that single score. 2 Be that as it may, such complaints are also deficient in terms of particulars, and do not properly reflect how the particular statute has been violated. Furthermore, no conceivable case even otherwise stands made out for the censure of the Respondent officials in the matter of such a single incident. Under such circumstances, while granting the application for urgency, we hereby dismiss the Petition in limine, along with the other miscellaneous application. JUDGE CHIEF JUSTICE MUBASHIR
